heading: Acoma Pueblo exchanges
label: 460uu–45 Acoma Pueblo exchanges

children.label: A
children.text: lands described in subsection (c) (with respect to trust lands west of New Mexico Highway 117); or

children.label: B
children.text: public lands of approximately equal value located outside the monument and outside the conservation area but within the boundaries of the State of New Mexico which are selected by the Pueblo of Acoma, so long as such exchange is consistent with applicable law and Bureau of Land Management resource management plans developed pursuant to the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 [ et seq.].43 U.S.C. 1701

All lands selected by and transferred to the Pueblo of Acoma at its request pursuant to this section shall thereafter be held in trust by the Secretary for the Pueblo of Acoma in the same manner as the lands for which they were exchanged.

Any lands west of New Mexico Highway 117 which are acquired by the Secretary pursuant to this section shall be incorporated into the monument and managed accordingly, and and all other provisions of this subchapter and other law applicable to lands designated by this subchapter as part of the monument shall apply to such incorporated lands.section 460uu–3 of this title

children.heading: Public lands available for exchange
children.label: b Public lands available for exchange
children.text: For purposes of acquiring lands pursuant to subsection (a) of this section, the Secretary, consistent with applicable law and Bureau of Land Management resource management plans described in subsection (a), shall make public lands within the boundaries of the State of New Mexico available for exchange. Nothing in this subchapter shall be construed as authorizing or requiring revocation of any existing withdrawal or classification of public land except in a manner consistent with applicable law.

children.heading: Public lands available for transfer; management of lands not exchanged or transferred
children.label: c Public lands available for transfer; management of lands not exchanged or transferred

children.text: The Secretary shall make the lands within the areas identified as “Acoma Potential Exchange Areas” on the map referenced in available for transfer to the Pueblo of Acoma pursuant to this subsection.section 460uu–21 of this title

Upon a request of the Pueblo of Acoma submitted to the Secretary no later than one year after , lands within the areas described in paragraph (1) shall be transferred to the Pueblo of Acoma in exchange for trust lands of approximately equal value within that portion of the conservation area west of New Mexico Highway 117. The Secretary may require exchanges of land under this subsection to be on the basis of compact and contiguous parcels.December 31, 1987

Any lands within the areas described in paragraph (1) not proposed for exchange by a request submitted to the Secretary by the Pueblo of Acoma within the period specified in paragraph (2), and any lands in such areas not ultimately transferred pursuant to this subsection, shall be incorporated within the conservation area and managed accordingly. In addition, any lands in that portion of the areas described in paragraph (1) lying in section 1, township 7N, range 9W, New Mexico Principal Meridian, not transferred to the Pueblo of Acoma pursuant to this subsection shall be added to and incorporated within the Cebolla Wilderness and managed accordingly.
